commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns Austria a 70% win probability versus the model's 44%, a 26-percentage-point gap favoring the stronger conviction in an Austria victory. The market also narrows the draw to 19% and Jordan's chances to 11%, while the model sees those outcomes as more plausible. This sharpening of confidence in Austria could reflect recent lineup news, injury updates, or current form that the model, built on older Elo data, does not yet incorporate. Conversely, the model's more balanced assessment may simply reflect structural uncertainty that the real-money market has resolved through information flow. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal of divergent confidence, not an adjudication of accuracy.
